rules.subject: torts
rules.name: Negligence elements
rules.statement: To recover for negligence, a plaintiff must prove duty, breach of that duty, actual causation, proximate causation, and damages.

rules.subject: torts
rules.name: Battery
rules.statement: Battery requires an intentional act by the defendant that causes a harmful or offensive contact with the plaintiff's person.

rules.subject: contracts
rules.name: Consideration
rules.statement: Consideration is defined as a bargained-for exchange in which each party incurs a legal detriment or confers a legal benefit.

rules.subject: evidence
rules.name: Hearsay definition
rules.statement: Hearsay is defined as an out-of-court statement offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted, and it is inadmissible unless an exception applies.

rules.subject: crim
rules.name: Miranda warnings
rules.statement: Before a custodial interrogation, officers must warn the suspect of the right to remain silent, that any statement may be used against him, the right to counsel, and the right to appointed counsel if he cannot afford one.

rules.subject: crim
rules.name: Common law murder
rules.statement: Common law murder is defined as the unlawful killing of another human being committed with malice aforethought.

rules.subject: realprop
rules.name: Adverse possession
rules.statement: Title by adverse possession requires possession that is actual, open and notorious, hostile, exclusive, and continuous throughout the statutory period.

rules.subject: conlaw
rules.name: Strict scrutiny
rules.statement: Under strict scrutiny, a law is upheld only if it is necessary to serve a compelling government interest, is narrowly tailored, and uses the least restrictive means available.
